expectations.text: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
expectations.evidence: outputs/ contains exactly one file: invoicing-dashboard-prototype.html (31,242 bytes). Grep of all src=/href= attributes shows only three external references, all CDN: https://cdn.tailwindcss.com (line 7) and two fonts.googleapis.com links (lines 8-9). No local css/js/asset paths are referenced; all styles are inline (lines 10-22) and all JS is in inline <script> blocks. No extra artifact files (screenshots/README) present in outputs/.

expectations.text: No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
expectations.evidence: grep -niE 'fetch\(|XMLHttpRequest|axios' over the file returns zero matches (exit 1). The only network references are the allowed CDN <script>/<link> tags on lines 7-9. All actions (markPaid, saveInvoice, sendDraft, demo*) operate purely on the in-memory `state` object.

expectations.text: Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
expectations.evidence: SEED_INVOICES (11 invoices, lines 33-67) and OVERFLOW_INVOICE (lines 70-76) are hardcoded constants; mutable copy lives in `state` (lines 79-86). Every interaction mutates state then calls render() which redraws stats/chart/tabs/list/drawer via innerHTML: markPaid (lines 316-323), saveInvoice push at line 509 + render() line 513, sendDraft (401-407), setFilter (246), demoEmpty/demoLoading/demoOverflow/demoReset (519-546). Grep confirms no location.reload or form submission anywhere.

expectations.text: The invoice list shows a status per invoice (e.g. draft/sent/paid/overdue)
expectations.evidence: renderList renders a Status column per row (line 301): `${STATUS_LABEL[inv.status]}` with badge colors for draft/sent/overdue/paid defined at lines 92-98. Seed data covers all four statuses (paid x7, overdue x1, sent x2, draft x1). The drawer header also shows the badge (line 362).

expectations.text: The create-invoice flow adds a new invoice to the visible list in memory
expectations.evidence: Click path traced: header 'New invoice' button (line 111, onclick="openModal()") -> modal with client/project/due/status fields and dynamic line items (renderModal, lines 421-484) -> 'Create invoice' button (line 480, onclick="saveInvoice()") -> saveInvoice (lines 494-514) builds an invoice object with id 'INV-00'+nextNumber, executes state.invoices.push(inv) (line 509), then render() (line 513) re-renders the list from state.invoices. Validation toasts guard empty client/items (lines 499-501).

expectations.text: Marking an invoice paid changes its status and the change is reflected in the UI (totals and/or chart update)
expectations.evidence: '✓ Mark paid' buttons exist in list rows for sent/overdue invoices (line 304) and in the drawer (line 392), both calling markPaid(id). markPaid (lines 316-323) sets inv.status='paid', inv.paidOn=TODAY ('2026-06-12'), then render(). renderStats recomputes Outstanding/Overdue/Collected-in-June from state (lines 176-179) and renderChart recomputes per-month paid sums (lines 201-205). Verified by node simulation: marking overdue INV-0048 paid moves $1,440 into the June chart bar (was $0) and reduces Outstanding accordingly.

expectations.text: A monthly revenue chart renders from the hardcoded data
expectations.evidence: renderChart (lines 200-234) builds a hand-rolled SVG bar chart over 8 months (Nov 2025-Jun 2026), summing invTotal of invoices with status==='paid' grouped by paidOn month prefix from state.invoices (which is seeded from the hardcoded SEED_INVOICES). It is called on initial render() at line 548 and injected into #chart (line 128). Bars carry $-value labels and the current month is highlighted.

expectations.text: An empty state (a list/screen with no data) is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Click path traced: fixed 'Demo states' panel button 'Empty' (line 152, onclick="demoEmpty()") -> demoEmpty (lines 519-524) sets state.invoices=[] and render() -> renderList's state.invoices.length===0 branch (lines 261-271) renders a dedicated empty state: document icon, 'No invoices yet', and a '＋ New invoice' CTA. A secondary per-filter empty message also exists (lines 273-275).

expectations.text: A loading state (spinner or skeleton) is reachable, e.g. simulated with setTimeout
expectations.evidence: Click path traced: 'Loading' demo button (line 153, onclick="demoLoading()") -> demoLoading (lines 525-529) sets state.loading=true, render() shows shimmer skeletons (CSS @keyframes shimmer + .skeleton, lines 14-15) in the chart area (lines 208-210) and five skeleton rows in the list (lines 252-255), then setTimeout(..., 1600) clears loading and re-renders.

expectations.text: An error message or toast is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Three click paths traced: (1) 'Error toast' demo button (line 154) -> demoError (lines 530-532) -> toast('error', "Couldn't sync invoices — connection lost..."); (2) drawer 'PDF ↓' button (line 396) -> downloadPdf (lines 329-332), a deliberate failure path showing an error toast; (3) saveInvoice validation errors (lines 499, 501). toast() renders error styling (rose border/text, ✕ icon) at lines 335-342.

expectations.text: An overflow case (too-long text or oversized value) is present so the user can see how the layout copes
expectations.evidence: OVERFLOW_INVOICE is hardcoded at lines 70-76: a ~150-char client name, a multi-line wall-of-text project description, and a $1,248,932,775.50 line item (verified via node: formats as $1,248,932,775.50). Click path: 'Overflow' demo button (line 155) -> demoOverflow (lines 533-540) pushes it into state.invoices and toasts instructions to open it. Layout coping is implemented: table cells use `truncate` with `overflow-hidden`/`table-fixed` (lines 280, 295-300), amount cell truncates (line 300), and the drawer uses `break-words` for client/project/item descriptions (lines 364-365, 380).

claims.claim: Header comment: 'ALL DATA LIVES HERE — hardcoded, mutated in memory only'
claims.evidence: All data originates from SEED_INVOICES/OVERFLOW_INVOICE constants; no storage, no network, all mutations on the in-memory state object.
months array (line 201) spans exactly 8 months (2025-11 through 2026-06) and only status==='paid' invoices are summed.

claims.claim: Overflow toast: 'a monster client name, wall-of-text project and a $1.2B amount'
claims.evidence: OVERFLOW_INVOICE line item is 1 x 1248932775.50 = $1,248,932,775.50 (~$1.2B), matching the toast's description.

suggestions.reason: All grading was done by static code reading plus node simulation of extracted logic; no assertion requires the page to actually load and run without JS errors in a browser. A template-literal or quoting bug (e.g. an unescaped double quote in user input breaking the value="..." attributes in renderModal) would pass every assertion here while breaking at runtime. Consider one assertion verified by opening the file in a browser (e.g. via Playwright) and checking the console is clean and the list renders N rows.

suggestions.assertion: An empty state (a list/screen with no data) is reachable by clicking
suggestions.reason: Satisfied via a dedicated 'Demo states' debug panel rather than an organic UI path. That is explicitly allowed by the grading rule (panel/seeded variant), but if the eval intends to test product-realistic state design, it could additionally require the empty state to also be reachable organically (e.g. a filter tab with zero matches), which this output happens to support too (lines 273-275).

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-chosen and discriminating: they forced hardcoded data, in-memory mutation, all four UI states, and an overflow case, and this output genuinely satisfies each with traceable click paths. The only gap is runtime verification in a real browser.
